title: Victoria Beckham Talks About Being a Good Parent After Family Disagreement
summary: Victoria Beckham has spoken out for the first time about a family disagreement with her son Brooklyn. She says she and her husband David have always tried to be the best parents they can be. Brooklyn posted on Instagram in January, saying his parents tried to ruin his relationship with his wife Nicola and that family love depends on promoting their public image. Victoria responded in an interview, explaining that they love their children and have tried to protect them for over 30 years in the public eye. She wished Brooklyn a happy birthday last month. Brooklyn's claims include that his mother canceled making Nicola's wedding dress and danced inappropriately at the wedding. Some guests agree with parts of his story, while others say it's not fully true. David Beckham hasn't commented directly but said children can make mistakes.

background_read: The Beckham family is famous worldwide. Victoria and David Beckham are celebrities who have been in the public eye for many years. They have four children, and Brooklyn is their oldest son. Families sometimes have disagreements, especially when they are famous and share their lives online. Social media like Instagram lets people post messages that can be seen by many others, which can lead to public arguments.
Article_Structure: WHO: Victoria and David Beckham, their son Brooklyn, and his wife Nicola.
WHAT: A family disagreement where Brooklyn accused his parents of trying to ruin his marriage and basing love on public image, and Victoria responded by saying they always tried to be good parents.
WHERE: The disagreement happened online on Instagram and in interviews, with events at a wedding.
WHY: To address public concerns about the family rift and explain their parenting efforts.

perspectives.description: She believes she and David have always tried to be the best parents, loving and protecting their children despite being in the public eye.
He feels his parents tried to harm his marriage and value public image over real family love.
